A01:2021-Broken Access Control moved up from fifth position; 94% of applications were tested for some form of broken access control.
Cryptographic Failures shift up to #2 in OWASP Top 10 2021, focusing on failures related to cryptography that lead to sensitive data exposure or system compromise.
Injection slides down to third position but still affects 94% of applications, with Cross-site Scripting now included in this category.
A04:2021-Insecure Design is a new category focusing on risks related to design flaws, calling for threat modeling and secure design patterns.
Security Misconfiguration moved up from #6; 90% of applications were tested for some form of misconfiguration.
Vulnerable and Outdated Components moves up from #9 in 2017, a known issue that is difficult to test and assess risk.
Identification and Authentication Failures slides down from second position, now including CWEs related to identification failures.
Software and Data Integrity Failures is a new category focusing on assumptions about software updates, critical data, and CI/CD pipelines without verifying integrity.
Server-Side Request Forgery is added from industry survey with above-average ratings for exploit and impact potential.
Without proper access control, an attacker can simply change the browser's 'acct' parameter to access any user's account via unverified data in SQL calls.
An attacker can force-browse target URLs requiring admin rights, such as changing the API endpoint to access admin pages.
Even with automatic database encryption, SQL injection can retrieve credit card details in clear text after decryption.

An e-commerce site vulnerable to bots allows scalpers to buy high-end video cards, generating negative publicity and long-term animosity.
A cinema booking flow without proper limits can be abused to book six hundred seats across multiple cinemas in a few requests.
Application servers with sample applications not removed from production allow attackers to compromise the server using known flaws.
Directory listing enabled on a server allows attackers to list directories and reverse engineer Java classes to find access control flaws.
Detailed error messages returned to users can expose sensitive information or underlying vulnerable component versions.

NoSQL injection involves bypassing authentication or extracting data by manipulating NoSQL database queries.
Server-Side Template Injection lets attackers inject template directives into templates to execute arbitrary code on the server.

Insecure Deserialization can lead to remote code execution by manipulating serialized objects.
Race conditions occur when timing-sensitive operations are not properly synchronized, allowing attackers to bypass logic.

JWT attacks exploit weaknesses in JSON Web Token implementations, including algorithm confusion and signature validation issues.
